data.title: Marvell rises as Citi resumes with Buy rating
data.description:
• Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rose 1% on Friday after investment firm Citi resumed coverage on the semiconductor company with a Buy rating.
  • “We believe Marvell will see accelerating Y/Y sales growth in 2026/27 driven by AI data centers,” Citi analyst Atif Malik wrote in a note to clients. “Marvell guided FY27/28 sales to grow ~20%/30% driven by multiple data center AI demand drivers. Moreover, the company has announced the acquisition of Celestial AI. We lift our FY26/27/28 EPS to $2.84/$3.56/$4.99 on higher data center sales outlook.” 
  • Malik also put a $114 price target on Marvell shares.

data.title: Virtus Investment Partners to acquire majority stake in Keystone National
data.description:
Virtus Investment Partners (VRTS) announced on Friday the acquisition of a majority interest in investment manager Keystone National Group for $200M at deal closing.

The transaction consideration also includes up to a $170M of deferred consideration, including earnout payments subject to the achievement of future revenue targets.

The acquisition is expected to be funded using existing balance sheet resources.

Keystone specializes in asset-centric private credit and also manages two private REITs specializing in real estate bridge lending and real estate equity.

The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Unity Software jumps on back of Wells Fargo upgrade
data.description:
• Unity Software (U) shares jumped 4.5% in premarket trading on Friday after Wells Fargo upgraded the video game software company. 
  • “We upgrade U to [Overweight from Equal-Weight] on 1) favorable industry backdrop in 2026 and 2) direct exposure to the direct payments migration w/'Unity Commerce,'” analyst Alec Brondolo wrote in a note to clients. “We raise FY26 grow segment rev growth to +17% y/y from +14% prior on faster industry growth. Unity announced 'Unity Commerce' 10/22 to facilitate the transition from app store to direct payments. We see U as well positioned vs. incumbents Xsolla, Appcharge & Aghanim. Now modeling $55M 2027 Unity Commerce revs, 8% upside [versus] our prior Create segment [revenues].”
  • Brondolo also raised his price target on Unity to $51 from $42.

data.title: SoftBank's Masayoshi Son plans to develop Trump Industrial Parks
data.description:
The Japanese billionaire tech investor Masayoshi Son, CEO of SoftBank, is reportedly mustering billions of dollars to build Trump-branded industrial parks in the United States after months of discussions with officials at the White House and Commerce Department.

The parks would be largely built on federal land, with funds pledged by the Japanese government as part of the recent trade deal, the Wall Street Journal reported.

The facilities would produce components for artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure.

President Donald Trump signalled that he supports the Son’s plan, the reported stated, according to people familiar with the matter.

SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY)(OTCPK:SFTBF) and the Trump administration are continuing the discussion.

According to people who attended the meetings, Son pitched Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and other U.S. officials on his plans to build a $1 trillion industrial city in the Arizona desert, north of Phoenix.

Over the summer, the SoftBank CEO pitched the project to Trump directly at a meeting, people familiar with the matter said.

However, the blueprint for the project changed after several meetings, according to an administration official, as the administration began pushing for a network of ‘Trump Industrial Parks' with manufacturing infrastructure built on federal land, instead of one mega development.

As per the current plans, softBank will help construct factory clusters that produce fiber-optic cables, data-center equipment and finally AI chips.

Japanese tech companies are expected to supply much of the expertise behind these new ventures, while the federal government will own the parks once built.  

An administration official reportedly stated that the initial funding for these projects could be released as early as the first quarter of 2026.

White House spokesperson Kush Desai reportedly said in a statement that President Trump’s relationship with business leaders across industries and geographies are playing a key role in securing trillions in investments that are helping build America’s next Golden Age.

As  part of the U.S.-Japan trade deal finalized in July, Japan is expected to invest $550 billion to build and expand core U.S. industries, with US retaining 90% of the profits from these investments.

Japan is expected, as per the fact sheet released by the US government, to invest in energy infrastructure, semiconductor manufacturing, critical mineral mining, and commercial and defense ship building.

Under the deal terms, the Trump administration agreed to limit tariffs to 15% from the previous 25% on imported Japanese goods and Japan agreed to open up trade in U.S. goods, including cars, trucks and agricultural products.

data.title: Rubrik rises as enterprises seek more cyber resilience in age of AI: analysts
data.description:
Rubrik's (RBRK) third-quarter financial results demonstrated the data security firm is continuing to gain momentum for enterprises due in part to the rising risks of artificial intelligence threats, according to analysts.

Shares had surged 18% by early trading on Friday.

"Cloud ARR came in at $1.175 billion (up 53% y/y), well above the prior period of $1.064 billion as more customers shift into cloud and GenAI while choosing RBRK for comprehensive cyber resilience," said Wedbush analysts, led by Dan Ives, in a Friday investor note. "Total revenue came in at $350.2 million which was well above both the Street’s estimate of $320.5 million and the company’s guidance range of $319.0 million - $321.0 million while non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 came in significantly above both the Street's ($0.17) estimate and guidance of ($0.18) - ($0.16) as the company continues to see strong demand with cloud migrations accelerating due to AI-driven enterprise modernization."

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and $120 price target on the stock.

"The company raised its FY26 guidance across the board, reflecting elevated demand as cyber resilience remains top of mind for CIOs and CISOs, with more organizations preparing for worst-case scenarios in the event of infrastructure breaches while AI continues to increase the threat level for enterprises," Ives added.

KeyBanc maintained its Overweight rating but slightly lowered its target price to $113 from $117 due to lower peer multiples. Meanwhile, William Blair upgraded the stock to Outperform from Market Perform due to its "stellar beat and raise."

Rubrik competitor Commvault Systems (CVLT) had inched down 0.2%.

"Our mission is to lead Rubrik into our next era as the, let me repeat, the security and AI operations company," said Rubrik CEO and co-founder Bipul Sinha, during the company's third-quarter earnings call. 

"Now with the rise of AI agents, a new transformation is underway," Sinha added. "AI transformation presents unique challenges. An agent operates within an organization's environment runs business processes, assumes identity, touches critical data and impacts mission-critical applications at unprecedented speed and scale. These agents are effectively superhuman, but organizations don't yet have the guardrails to monitor and contain what they do. We need to think about AI agents very differently. Through this AI transformation, Rubrik's mission and its strategy remains the same: to accelerate our customers' journey to AI enterprise."

data.title: ServiceTitan pops as Piper Sandler praises after strong Q3 results, guidance
data.description:
• ServiceTitan (TTAN) was in focus on Friday after the trade industry software company reported stronger-than-expected third-quarter results, leading to praise from Piper Sandler.
  • Shares rose more than 6% in premarket trading.
  • “TTAN posted another quarter of healthy 25% y/y revenue growth on an $11M beat to the midpoint guide, driven by continued commercial and Pro product growth and usage revenue upside from strong GTV results and greater-than-expected fintech attach,” Piper Sandler analyst Hannah Rudoff wrote in a note to clients. “We see upside to conservative Q4 growth guidance that implies further deceleration to 17% y/y in Q4, based on a robust demand environment (including increased appetite to adopt AI by the trades) and encouraging process across key growth vectors (e.g. commercial suite that is now end- to-end with the addition of Commercial CRM and construction management capabilities, Max program that should drive increase Pro product attach). TTAN remains well positioned as the platform-of-choice for the trades, with the potential to evolve into a secular AI winner.”
  • Rudoff maintained her Overweight rating on ServiceTitan but lowered her price target to $140 from $155.

data.title: Academy Sports and Outdoors declares $0.13 dividend
data.description:
• Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO) declared $0.13/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 1.05%
  • Payable Jan. 15; for shareholders of record Dec. 18; ex-div Dec. 18.
  • See ASO Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: West Fraser to curtail High Level OSB mill, take $200M charge
data.description:
West Fraser Timber (WFG) said late Thursday it plans to indefinitely curtail its oriented strand board mill in High Level, Alberta, in Spring 2026, affecting 190 employees.

West Fraser (WFG) said the decision is the result of a significant weakening of OSB demand, and is expected to reduce the company's total capacity by 860 million sq ft.

The company said it expects to record a ~$200 million asset impairment loss in Q4 due to the reductions at High Level.

West Fraser (WFG) also confirmed it will continue to idle one of its production lines at its OSB mill in Cordele, Georgia, which has been offline since late 2023.

Oriented strand board is used in residential construction, repair, and remodelling and industrial applications.

data.title: HoldCo takes $140M stake in KeyCorp, pushes for CEO exit - report
data.description:
Hedge fund HoldCo Asset Management has taken a $140M stake in KeyCorp (KEY), and is pushing for the removal of the Cleveland, OH-based lender's CEO, the Financial Times reported on November 5.

KEY shares were -0.26% Friday pre-market to $19.06.

HoldCo has reportedly launched its fifth activist campaign of 2025 targeting U.S. banks.

Founders Ghei and Zaitzeff are pushing KeyCorp to use its excess capital to buy back stock, adopt a "no acquisitions" policy, and removal of CEO Chris Gorman and board member Alexander Cutler, the FT reported, citing a 50-page presentation by the activist investor addressed to the bank's board.

The Florida-based activist investor was said to have been pushing Comerica (CMA) for sale and was seeking board seats in early September.

In early October, Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) struck an all-stock deal to acquire Comerica.

data.title: New York Times sues Perplexity AI for copyright infringement - report
data.description:
The New York Times (NYT) is suing Perplexity AI for copyright infringement, claiming the AI startup is taking content from the newspaper – including videos, podcasts and images -- and repackaging it “verbatim or near-verbatim” in its search results. 

In the company’s lawsuit filed in a New York federal court, the New York Times accuses Perplexity of “violat[ing] the protections that intellectual property law provides for The Time’s expressive, original journalism.” The lawsuit also includes Perplexity’s Comet AI assistant for producing detailed summaries of articles published by the NYT.

The lawsuit is the latest attempt by the newspaper to force Perplexity to stop using its content. Last October, the Times issued a cease and desist notice to Perplexity, followed by another notice this July.

The New York Times (NYT) is seeking damages and injunctive relief, including the removal of NYT content from Perplexity products.

Perplexity is also being sued by Dow Jones, publisher of The Wall Street Journal, The Chicago Tribune, and the New York Post (NWSA) for similar copyright infringement.
